Bulk-link Parents to Students (Excel)

For linking many Parent/Student accounts at once. Each row: a Username (must already exist as a Parent/Student account) and one or more Student ID(s) (the "STU-####" ID shown in Student Database exports) — separate multiple IDs in one cell with a semicolon.

☁ Firebase Sync

All data entered in this system is saved live to a shared Firestore database, so it stays backed up and instantly synced across every device/browser that opens this page — no manual push/pull needed.

✓ Connected to project —. Changes made on this device sync automatically, and changes made elsewhere appear here in real time.

🕒 Activity & Login Log

Every sign-in and every change made in the system — who did it, and when.

Date & TimeUserActionDetails

🚫 Blocked Students

Check any student to block their linked Parent/Student account from the whole system. Blocked accounts will see "Account is NOT active" instead of any tab, right after logging in (Admin only).

🗓 Term & Month Dates

Set the Start and End date of the 1st and 2nd Month for each Term (Admin only). Set once — it will not change automatically, and stays visible to every user until the Admin updates it again.

Term 1

Term 2

Friday and Saturday are always excluded automatically. Any holiday dates added here apply to every Section, Stage, Grade, Class and Subject for that Term's Month — no need to set them again per class.

📅 Exam Schedule

Set the Date, Day, Subject, Time (From/To), Duration and Room for each exam (Admin only). Visible to every user until the Admin updates it again.

Reference layout — the MILS Examination Card & Schedule design used for print.

🎫 Seat & Committee Assignments (Admin only)

Upload each student's Seat Number, Committee No., Floor, Section and Room No. for this exam. Each Parent/Student account will only see their own child's card.

Session data is stored temporarily in the browser only — use the "Full Backup" button to save all data to your device, and "Restore Backup" to continue your work later.

1

Start by selecting the Academic Term, Section, Stage, Grade, Class, Mark Entry and Subject

Use the steps above in order to display the grade sheet for the class.

Friday and Saturday are automatically excluded from every attendance table, along with any holiday dates you add above. Absence is recorded separately for each Subject — tick a box to mark a student absent on that day — the Total column adds up all recorded absence days for this Month, and automatically updates that student's "Beh. & Attend." grade for that same Subject, for the matching Term and Month in the Grade Book. You can still edit that grade by hand afterwards at any time. Checking "Apply to ALL Sections, Stages, Grades & Classes" before creating/updating the table sets the same date range and excluded days for every class that teaches this Subject at once, instead of one class at a time.

1

Start by selecting the Academic Term, Section, Stage, Grade, Class, Subject and Month

Use the steps above in order to display the attendance sheet for the class.

2

Select Section, Stage, Grade and Term to view this report

Use the steps above in order to display the report for the class.

Generate Class Lists

Use the steps above to select Section, Stage, Grade and Class, then view the class list with student information.

Students
0 students

To add a new student, go to the "Grade Book" tab and select the Section, Stage and Grade first, then click "Add Student".
From here you can search and filter across all classes, edit each student's second language, and delete students.

Teachers Database
0 teachers
Hold Ctrl (Cmd on Mac) to select multiple classes

Add teachers one by one with the "Add Teacher" button, or bulk-import them all at once from an Excel/CSV sheet using "Bulk Import from Excel" (download the blank template first to see the required columns: ID, Teachers Name, Section, Subject, Classes).

Import Complete

📤 Upload Grades by Item

Pick which mark-entry item you want to fill in from an Excel sheet. Only that item will be updated for the students already in this class list — everything else on their record stays untouched.

The file needs a ID or Student Name column to identify each student, plus a column with the item's own name (e.g. "Month 1 Q. 1") or a column titled Score. The downloadable template already has the right columns and current class list, so it's the easiest way to start. Rows that don't match any student in the current list are skipped and listed after the upload.

حذف الطلاب

اختر الطريقة التي تريدها:

📊 Bulk Import Grades by Subject(s)

Import grades for one subject, multiple subjects, or all subjects at once from an Excel file.

You can select 2 or more subjects. Your Excel file should have columns for Student ID/Name and each selected subject.

📌 Import grades for 0 subjects in one Excel file.

Your file should have columns: Student ID (or Student Name), then one column per subject with the subject name as the header.

File format: First column should be Student ID or Student Name. For Single mode: one score column. For Multiple/All modes: each column header should match a subject name exactly. Only matching students will be updated; non-matching rows are reported after upload.
